programme.desc: We’ve all heard headlines heralding the meat and dairy industry as the villains of the climate crisis, but is this really true?

programme.title: Cornwall's Climate Stories
programme.desc: Today's industrialised food system causes a huge amount of our carbon emissions - and we waste one-third of all the food produced.

programme.title: Skydance
programme.desc: Follow Laura, a young journalist, and Alec, an ex-fighter pilot, as they journey the final leg of a prestigious aerial race across France

programme.title: Space Junk
programme.desc: After 50 years of launching into space, a troubling legacy casts a shadow over the future of space exploration

programme.title: Wonders of the Arctic
programme.desc: Ice is to the Arctic as trees are to the rainforest. It is the underpinning of the ecosystem

programme.title: Otters and the Exotic Pet Trade
programme.desc: Tracking the exotic pet trade of otters across the globe, is a story of gangsters and guns, mammals, and middlemen. Japanese otter cafés and social media influencers are driving increased demand for pet otters all over the world, with devastating consequences. These otters might be adorable, but the truth behind this trade is anything but. Ripped from the wild or irresponsibly bred, orphaned by poachers and confined in unnatural environments, these otters live a life of extreme misery. Following World Animal Protection intelligence, environmental photojournalist Aaron Gekoski investigates Otters and the Exotic Pet Trade.

programme.title: Waking the T. Rex: The Story of Sue
programme.desc: In 1990, deep in the badlands of South Dakota, paleontologists unearthed the bones of a monster

programme.title: Ice Dogs
programme.desc: The Arctic is accessible to man only because of ice dogs

programme.title: Green China Rising
programme.desc: In the war to save our planet's future, the battleground is China.

programme.title: Paseka: The Easter Elephant
programme.desc: On Easter morning, a baby elephant is mysteriously abandoned by her herd in the wilds of Botswana's Okavango Delta. Saved from certain death by a bizarre chain of events, she forces herself into a very unusual, captive elephant herd. This is "Paseka's" story. We follow her first year in this spectacular African wilderness, learning daily what it means to be an elephant. What is instinctive? What is she taught by the elephants? What does she need to learn from the humans? How does her arrival change the herd dynamics? Her unique journey is our window into the elephant world.

programme.title: Iberia's Natural Border
programme.desc: Tagus International is a protected natural space shared between Spain and Portugal

programme.title: Great Ocean Love
programme.desc: Follow Belinda Baggs, Linley Hurrell, and Lilly Pollard on a journey of discovery along the Great Ocean Road.

programme.title: Greenwood Is Still Burning
programme.desc: Learn about the events of the 1921 Tulsa Race Massacre and the ongoing legacy of harm it continues to cause the Greenwood community.

programme.title: Keep Cool
programme.desc: In the aftermath of devastating wildfire seasons, a remarkable effort emerges to restore the scorched lands of British Columbia.

programme.title: In Love with a Problem
programme.desc: Miranda Wang and Jeanny Yao are two millennial innovators embarking on an inspiring journey to solve the problem of plastic waste.

programme.title: Interbeing
programme.desc: Interbeing is a multimedia project which celebrates traditional music and the beauty of the landscape in Romania - a way of life and landscape that is under threat.

programme.title: Grounded
programme.desc: In the rugged mountains of western Montana, a team of researchers makes a chilling discovery: the golden eagles they study are dying from a mysterious illness.

programme.title: Play Hungry
programme.desc: In a country consisting of over 7500 islands and 175 languages, the Philippines is as diverse and disunited as it gets. But the one thing that ties it all together is the love for the game - basketball!

programme.title: Play On: Football’s Climate Story
programme.desc: The world’s most popular sport is under threat. Climate change is threatening to disrupt football at all levels - from grassroots right to the top tier.

programme.title: MATAR
programme.desc: Based on the lived experience of co-writer Ayman Alhussein, this film follows the story of an asylum seeker in England who is forced to live on the fringes of society and rely on his bike to survive.

programme.title: Raised by Sand & Salty Water
programme.desc: This documentary explores Wafa's inspiring life story and how skateboarding has shaped her journey, highlighting the broader influence of skateboarding and surfing in Arabic culture.

programme.title: ReCoral
programme.desc: ReCoral by Ørsted is a proof-of-concept coral restoration trial in partnership with the Penghu Marine Biology Research Center.

programme.title: Mahale Chimp Guardians
programme.desc: This is the story of the Greater Mahale ecosystem and its importance as a biodiversity hotspot.

programme.title: Mi Refugio
programme.desc: Refuge is the place where we go to be safe and at peace. More and more people feel the sea as that refuge.

programme.title: People of the Ice Bridge
programme.desc: The North Water, or Pikialasorsuaq, is one of the most productive ocean ecosystems on the planet and has been a vital resource for indigenous communities for millennia.

programme.title: Mission Blue: Harbouring Hope
programme.desc: The oceans are the source of life on Earth, yet despite their vastness they are in trouble. Mission Blue, a Perpetual Planet Initiative partner, ignites change through a global network of “Hope Spots”, marine-protected areas of critical vulnerability.

programme.title: Cocaine Hippos
programme.desc: Originally Pablo Escobar's playthings, more than one hundred African hippos are now an invasive species in Colombia. Can a global team of conservationists, lawyers, and even local residents find a way to save these unwanted guests?

programme.title: Challenge the Gap with Paul Black
programme.desc: Challenge the Gap with Paul Black is a fun yet investigative social short following the Glaswegian Tik-Tok star on a journey of discovery as he meets innovative businesses in his home city who are leading the way when it comes to shifting towards a circular economy. Helping to break down barriers on the topic of circularity, Paul will undertake a series of challenges inspired by the local businesses he'll meet. Supported by the Glasgow Chamber of Commerce, our film champions those who are challenging the traditional, linear methods of creation, design and action, and sets out why we must all join the fight to help close the circularity gap.

programme.title: Dancing on Icebergs
programme.desc: In 2018 Kiwi choreographer Corey Baker made history when he filmed a world first; a dance film to highlight the impact of climate change on Antarctica

programme.title: Sharks: The Big Five
programme.desc: The ocean has its own Big Five. Featuring great whites, to hammerheads, tigers, bulls, and the intimidating whale shark

programme.title: Jack Wolfskin Presents A Wild Education
programme.desc: The extraordinary journey of a family living deep in the wild of the Carpathian Mountains.

programme.title: La Loma Viva | The Living Hill
programme.desc: ‘The Living Hill’ tracks the transitional period of Karen and Ryan’s exodus from the city to living off the land in rural Andalusia. A period of sensory resetting, agricultural experimentation and ultimately a journey that builds a bond with nature.

programme.title: In Death Is Life
programme.desc: In Death is Life is a short film about the tensions and interconnections of people and peatlands in rural Ireland.

programme.title: Just a Kid from Baltimore
programme.desc: Evan Gill traded the tough streets of Baltimore for a different kind of climb: conquering mountains. But his journey wasn't just about reaching the peak.

programme.title: It’ll Never Catch On: The Veganuary Story
programme.desc: Founders Matthew Glover and Jane Land look back over 10 years of Veganuary, with team members and celebrity ambassadors including Evanna Lynch, Kellie Bright, Chris Packham, Peter Egan, Benjamin Zephaniah and Jasmine Harman shining a light on how it has become the cultural phenomenon it is today.

programme.title: Greenfingers
programme.desc: In the wake of dire scientific warnings, protestors take a stand for a better tomorrow, their voices rising against the entrenched interests of industry.

programme.title: In a State of Change
programme.desc: An American photographer in Iceland questions whether beauty can be an effective tool for raising awareness and inspiring action on climate change.

programme.title: Island in the Sky
programme.desc: Join the native tribes of the Conglomerate Mesa as they defend their heritage and culture against extractive industries.

programme.title: Javier Bardem in the Antarctic with Greenpeace
programme.desc: Join Javier Bardem on a journey to one of earth’s final frontiers: the Antarctic ocean.

programme.title: Hope Restored
programme.desc: In a world where coral reefs are facing a bleak future, there is a ray of hope shining from Bonaire in the Dutch Caribbean.

programme.title: How Water Makes Climate
programme.desc: Learn about the crucial connection between water cycles and the climate.

programme.title: Held by the Water
programme.desc: Wild swimmer Fiona isn't just taking a dip, she's reclaiming her life.

programme.title: Wild Hope
programme.desc: All around the world, seabirds provide a critical link between land and sea. On Hawai’i, ecologists are working to protect two vital shearwater species that helped life first take hold across these islands.

programme.title: Curupira
programme.desc: The story of a powerful Amazonian goddess told through the voices of the Borarí people from the heart of the forest in Pará, Brazil.

programme.title: Broken Wings
programme.desc: The bird, a symbol of freedom, is being poached at a pace never seen before.

programme.title: Broken Wings
programme.desc: The bird, a symbol of freedom, is being poached at a pace never seen before.

programme.title: Encouraged
programme.desc: Courage Adams has taken the BMX world by storm. Follow him as he returns to his roots in Nigeria.

programme.title: Belonging: A Journey of Inclusion and Nature
programme.desc: We follow a group of students taking part in GCST 1970, an innovative program designed to immerse urban youth in nature-based education and environmental justice.

programme.title: Diving the 65th Parallel
programme.desc: Join Greenpeace International on an extraordinary mission to the Weddell Sea in Antarctica.

programme.title: Circumnavigate
programme.desc: Father of three and former lifeguard Brendon Prince from South Devon attempts to become the first person to stand-up paddleboard around mainland Britain - a challenge many have tried but none have achieved.

programme.title: Before the Oil
programme.desc: We will need more energy while at the same time we want to phase out our oil. Deep in the mountains in southern Norway, Odd Kvinen, who runs a husky farm, faces an energy transition challenge.

programme.title: For the Next Generation
programme.desc: In Montana, the Confederated Salish and Kootenai Tribes have been working for generations to restore and preserve the biodiversity on their reservation that includes their tribal wilderness in the Mission Mountain Range.

programme.title: Follow the Wind
programme.desc: Jerrie van de Kop follows the wind as he kiteboards from warm waters to snowy peaks.

programme.title: Extract. Destroy. Repeat
programme.desc: Set in the far-off dystopian future of... 2023. Extract. Destroy. Repeat is a short animation following our unlikely hero Stan, during his induction day at the deep-sea mining company, Happy Metals Corp.

programme.title: Finding Solo
programme.desc: Amongst the hustle and bustle of Kuala Lumpur city lies an isolated forest, home to one of our closest living relatives.

programme.title: Garden Ministry
programme.desc: A joyful portrait of Reverend Dallas Conyers and her passion for healing the climate crisis through community organizing and her front yard garden.

programme.title: Forgotten I
programme.desc: Balinese people have to settle with toxic water to fulfill their daily needs while a group of driven individuals is at work to find solutions.

programme.title: Forgotten Il
programme.desc: Vast development is creating a major issue for Jakarta: land is now equated with money rather than an essential need of every human being.

programme.title: Frontier Town
programme.desc: By 2054, Fairbourne will be decommissioned due to rising tides. This is a film about the people labelled “the UK’s first climate refugees”.

programme.title: As We Grow
programme.desc: Follow the journey of two young organic vegetable farmers from Scotland as they embark on a transformative adventure to the Peruvian jungle.

programme.title: 41 Degrees North
programme.desc: Immerse yourself in the tranquil rhythms of Shenyang, China. Shot over seven weeks, this observational documentary captures the essence of daily life without political undertones.

programme.title: Apayauq
programme.desc: Follow the story of a young Alaskan woman named Apayauq Reitan, who in 2022 became the first openly transgender musher to compete in the Iditarod, a grueling dog sled race that covers more than 1,000 miles through the Alaskan wilderness.

programme.title: Rivers of Hope
programme.desc: Stories from remote areas in Bangladesh bring us close to the lives of families who are most vulnerable to climate shifts. Bangladesh is a water country and life here is lived among the spaces between. Land is constantly being shifted and swallowed by water. Yet water means access to livelihood and it means survival.

programme.title: GuardianesDeLaSelva_subtitles
programme.desc: GuardianesDeLaSelva_subtitles

programme.title: Aquarium: An Aquatic Life
programme.desc: Join us as we delve into the inner workings of the Two Oceans Aquarium and meet the team who keep the machine running.

programme.title: Aquarium: An Aquatic Life
programme.desc: This episode will introduce you to some of the Aquarium's most weird and wonderful inhabitants.

programme.title: Aquarium: An Aquatic Life
programme.desc: Come and meet one of the most popular attractions of the Two Oceans Aquarium: the party of penguins!

programme.title: Aquarium: An Aquatic Life
programme.desc: Ever wondered how to look after dozens of tiny turtles?

programme.title: Aquarium: An Aquatic Life
programme.sub-title: S01 : E05 Return of the Sharks
programme.desc: In the aftermath of Cape Town's biggest storm in 2 decades, there’s a whole lot of work to be done at the Two Oceans Aquarium!

programme.title: Follow the Wind
programme.desc: Jerrie van de Kop follows the wind as he kiteboards from warm waters to snowy peaks.

programme.title: Carne: The Unsustainable Footprint
programme.desc: A gentle call for a cease-fire at the table, with inspiring stories of how a diet change can help the planet, animals and human health

programme.title: Frontier Town
programme.desc: Fairbourne’s residents have been told that by 2054 their village will be decommissioned and left to the sea.

programme.title: Keep Cool
programme.desc: In the aftermath of devastating wildfire seasons, a remarkable effort emerges to restore the scorched lands of British Columbia.

programme.title: Cocaine Hippos
programme.desc: Originally Pablo Escobar's playthings, more than one hundred African hippos are now an invasive species in Colombia. Can a global team of conservationists, lawyers, and even local residents find a way to save these unwanted guests?

programme.title: Play Hungry
programme.desc: In a country consisting of over 7500 islands and 175 languages, the Philippines is as diverse and disunited as it gets. But the one thing that ties it all together is the love for the game - basketball!

programme.title: Spirit of the Forest
programme.desc: In the verdant embrace of Nucșoara, Romania’s ancient beech trees stand as silent witnesses to history, unique wildlife, and a community’s resilience.

programme.title: On the Trails of the Glaciers
programme.desc: 100 years after the expedition of Duke of the Abruzzi to the Baltoro glacier, a team follow in his footsteps.

programme.title: On the Trails of the Glaciers
programme.desc: Fabiano Ventura and his team continue to study the effects of climate change on the world’s largest glaciers.

programme.title: On the Trails of the Glaciers
programme.desc: Glaciers are found in Alaska at low altitudes, passing through the long valleys before they arrive at the sea.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Dangerous and Deadly I
programme.desc: Wildlife film-makers often risk life and limb trying to capture unique images of their dangerous protagonists.

programme.title: Wild Moments
programme.sub-title: S01 : E02 Dangerous and Deadly II
programme.desc: Follow wildlife-filmmakers on their adventurous journeys throughout the world.

programme.title: Storm Hunters
programme.desc: Adventurer George Kourounis’ patience is tested as he encounters deadly wildlife and challenging elements while preparing to descend into Marum Volcano in the South Pacific.

programme.title: Storm Hunters
programme.desc: Mark and Jaclyn make their annual trek to Tornado Alley and this time they are looking for more than severe thunderstorms and tornadoes. They also want answers as to whether Climate Change is causing changes to Tornado Alley.

programme.title: Storm Hunters
programme.desc: Doubt and disbelief plague the Stormhunters after an exhaustive chase through Tornado Alley. The team is forced to make an uncomfortable decision. Do they play it safe or push past their fear?

programme.title: Mission: Hurricane

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Maud Urbas
programme.desc: It is a disease, managed for years by ineffective drugs, which brings Maud to consult a naturopath. This meeting will be the trigger for a revelation, a new choice of life turned to the well being of the other through coaching. Follow Maud's changes every day to turn to her well-being.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Ika
programme.desc: After graduating with a Law degree, Ika built a career as a legal manager and advisor. For a decade she worked on risk-related matters. One day, she decided to take a big calculated risk of her own and give up her law-life to pursue a passion for jewelry-making. Her creations are inspired by her travels and the natural materials surrounding her.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Hansen
programme.desc: A former model and Sports News anchor, Hansen has drifted away from the limelight to focus on a personal journey. His Yoga trip began 10 years ago, after suffering a herniated disc injury. His experiences have taught him that the only way one can truly change the world is to raise the consciousness of humanity, and this actually starts within ourselves.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Mark, Camille & Juliana
programme.desc: A Filipino couple with a shared love for traveling decide to change their lives and explore a passion for eco-tourism. Their glamping tents are perched on the hills of Bacuit Bay and promise a natural and enriching experience. Daily sunset yoga sessions and Farm-to-Table dining are just some of the activities they share with their guests. Their energy attracts like-minded people on similar journeys, notably their yoga teacher Juliana who has an inspiring story of her own to share with us.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Lille country
programme.desc: Head north to discover a French city that cannot be ignored: Lille. The city has an exceptional architectural heritage, a merchant town since the Middle Ages, a citadel under Louis XIV, an industrial town in the 19th century... it expanded over the centuries. Julie will meet those who contribute to the fame of the city and its region, through its culinary heritage with the menu of this journey: the recipe of the coq à la bière, that of the hochepot and for a sweet note, a crème brûlée with chicory.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The flavors of olive oil
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The flavors of olive oil
programme.desc: Julie is offering you a trip to the south of France. Between the Rhone Valley and the Mediterranean, let's discover a fruit that is harvested in November: the olive. On the way, we will stop at the Sourdon family's house to cook sea bream accompanied by an olive fricot. We will then go to Aix en Provence to prepare a strange dessert: the Fernand-style oil pump. Then we head for an exceptional estate, the Villa Beaulieu, to taste a Bagna Cauda like you have rarely seen before finishing with Jean-Luc Rabanel, a starred organic chef and defenders of grand cru olive oil...

programme.desc: The oceans cover more than 70% of the Earth's surface. The sea is the largest natural environment on our planet and remains virtually unexploited. This Canadian with the physique of an adventurer has taken up the defence of marine animals. In Corsica, in the Bouches de Bonifacio, the Mediterranean basin is one of the richest in biodiversity on the planet. In Corsica, with overfishing. Worldwide, 75% of fish stocks are threatened by overfishing. The cause? Large-scale, wasteful and insufficiently regulated fishing. In...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Turtle Conservatory Center
programme.desc: Breathtaking views, quality products and two impressive initiatives. That's what is ready for you in this episode. A first step takes us to Fabien's winery, 'the Ojai Vineyard'. This place in the heart of Ojai's nature combines the best of California production with an unexpected 'French touch' by bringing his equipment from France. We then visit 'The Turtle Conservatory Center', a reserve where rare species of turtles gather from around the world. Respectful of their needs and living conditions, this center is a haven of peace for turtles and Val, who will definitely come back here on her day off.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Zoe Boivin
programme.desc: To know how to listen, it can take a long time as it can happen in a revelation. It's a real revelation that led Zoe to listen to herself, to hear a destiny that dedicated her to an artistic career. From her studio to a vernissage, from her family to a workshop given in a school, share Zoe's adventure to the path that truly corresponds to her.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Claire
programme.desc: One woman’s personal mission to reduce her household rubbish turned into a passion that gave birth to Southeast Asia’s first Zero-Waste Bulk Food shop. Claire is a driven and successful mother of three who turned away from her corporate job to channel her business-savvy into reducing our impact on the environment and contributing to the improvement of her local community.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Zaira (Argentina)
programme.desc: For a long time Zaira let herself be defined solely as a survivor of the traumatic sexual assault that marked her life. In her healing search, she left behind the busy city life and moved to seek solace in the mountains of Mendoza. It is among these magnificent peaks that Zaira found peace within herself and harmony with her surroundings. Now, eager to help others heal, she is the leader of a trekking initiative that doubles as a support group for other women who are victims of sexual aggression.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Ile (Argentina)
programme.desc: Ileana studied business and worked long hours indoors for a multinational logistics company. Her love for dance and desire for movement drove her to quit her corporate life and begin a new chapter as a fitness trainer. Quickly exhausting the traditional methods available at her local gyms, she began to create innovative and groundbreaking exercise methods of her own. Her goal: to empower women by making them feel strong and feminine at the same time.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The Country of Le Mans
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The Country of Le Mans
programme.desc: It is in the heart of the Sarthe that Julie begins her new tour. Located in the Pays de la Loire region, the Sarthe department is the gateway to the French Grand Ouest. In the Haut Maine region, Le Mans is a city of art as well as a historic city, which played a decisive role in the development of the automobile. The country of Le Mans is distinguished by the diversity of its heritage, having kept in its stones the imprints of all eras. This region is notably renowned for its poulet de Loué, its capon and many other regional specialities. Let's go for a real Sarthe gourmet journey, with veal rouelles à la mancelle, roast poularde and seasonal fruit tart.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Seashells on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Seashells on the menu
programme.desc: We will start this journey near La Rochelle to learn a little more about the breeding of the famous bouchot mussels but also a very particular technique to cook them. We will then go on a tour of France's best oysters: towards Cancale, Arcachon, Montpellier and finally Oléron to learn about the different breeding methods and to find out more about all the species in France. Julie will cook a fricassee of berniques, the little Chinese hats that cover the rocks of the coast, as well as pink clams that are little known to the public. We will continue our journey in Finistère to meet Sylvain, the only abalone breeder in the country, and then to Méru where there are still workshops magnifying...

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - David Jeannerot
programme.desc: David Jeannerot left the world of fashion and abandoned his career as artistic director to become a plant. An exceptional florist who wishes to give back all its place to nature in the city.David had a dream life. He traveled around the world for major ready-to-wear brands. But under the appearance David was actually very isolated. To give meaning to his life, and to go in the direction of his personal convictions, he decides to become a plantist. He arranges corners of greenery on the smallest window sill with the aim of bringing nature to the city, and to make Paris greener.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Agostina (Argentina)
programme.desc: When Agostina’s mother was diagnosed with breast cancer, she began seeking out soft natural fabrics that would not irritate her mother’s sensitive skin. She found it almost impossible to find the right materials, so she decided to make her own. Research led her to bamboo fibers and she realised she could make a positive impact on the environment, while developing sustainable and ethical fashion.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Solenn (Nantes)
programme.desc: A former school teacher reinvents herself when she discovers a passion for sharing other women’s stories. Always inspired by the journeys of strong women, Solenn began a blog to celebrate the women in her community. Solenn’s energy and passion fueled a dream of starting her own magazine. Thanks to a successful crowdfunding campaign, this dream blossomed into a professional career in journalism and she launched a print mook (magazine & book) called Etonnantes. Etonnantes features interviews of women of all backgrounds, celebrating their diversity and success in each field.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Sophie (Nantes)
programme.desc: Always an artist at heart, Sophie grew up in the countryside amongst nature and farm animals. Her love for creating art while respecting nature turned into a drive to seek out sustainable vegetable dyes.Today, her artwork and craft are intertwined as she makes beautiful prints and motifs that she colours with natural dyes, prepared from handpicked variety of flowers and plants that turn into stunning creations.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The Bordeaux country
programme.desc: Julie meets us in Bordeaux after having surveyed the Blaye region, visited the Basin twice and travelled through the Libourne region twice, it was necessary to stop in the capital of Bordeaux one day! What better time than September to discover the pearl of Aquitaine, more often called the "City of Wine"? Julie invites the inhabitants to discover the traditions but also the more recent projects of this city full of history and gastronomy.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Poultry on the menu
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Poultry on the menu
programme.desc: Julie heads for Burgundy to discover ancient breeds of poultry reintroduced to France thanks to the work of breeder Fred Ménager. We'll then move on to Auvergne to discover how the famous Label Rouge is awarded and the role of certifying bodies ... because it's important to know what ends up on our plates. Julie also travels to the Drôme to cook guinea fowl, before heading to the Périgord to prepare a turkey stew. Julie will then head back up towards the center to discover an amazing fly maker who uses rooster feathers!

programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 1 - The Earth does not belong to Man, Man belongs to the Earth - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The Earth does not belong to Man, Man belongs to the Earth - Part 1
programme.desc: We end up at Yellowstone National Park in the United States, it is located in the first natural park created in the world. In Peru, the Amazon rainforest is suffering from massive deforestation. The desert on Beijing's doorstep is regularly hit by sandstorms. Some experts predict that the Chinese capital could be threatened by deserts within a hundred years. In Montana, American agricultural productivity has an ecological and human cost. Engineer Marc Dufumier explains how mechanisation is undermining the natural balance. In Brazil, agrofuels have made it possible to reduce dependence on oil, cut urban air pollution and even limit greenhouse gas emissions. In Los Angeles, human activities...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Chocolate Tan
programme.desc: Never thought yoga and chocolate could be a healthy combination? You were wrong! Since everything is organic in California, chocolate tanning is the best way to stay glamorous while taking care of your skin with natural and delicious products. After testing this chocolate tan, we'll be sweating it out at Tantris, the three-story yoga studio in the heart of Hollywood.

programme.title: Escape
programme.desc: Watch beautiful landscapes from all over the world !

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 1 - Episode 2
programme.desc: In this episode, you will discover beautiful landscapes from the point of view of a surfer. Check the view from the sky with a team of parachutist. Help a kayaker to navigate through the forest and take a zip line road over another forest. Don’t miss these hearthtaking landscapes !

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 1 - Episode 3
programme.desc: In this episode, take a break to watch this paradise beach view with a jogger, and fly with a parasail. Kayak between icebergs, snowboard between the trees and let yourself be tempted by a golf game in the sun. Don’t miss these hearthtaking landscapes !

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 1 - Episode 4
programme.desc: In this episode, face the waves aboard a catamaran, surf on the water using high speed water ski. Let yourself be tempted by fishing in the moutain on a clear water, and face the fear of the void on the edge of the Grand Canyon. Don’t miss these hearthtaking landscapes !

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 1 - Episode 5
programme.desc: In this episode, explore the deep of the ocean near the coast to discover exotic fishes, in a very clear water, with an oxygen bottle and a diving mask. Don’t miss these hearthtaking landscapes !

programme.title: Minute Bowl - Season 1 - Antioxidant smoothie
programme.desc: In this episode we decided to make an antioxidant smoothie bowl. For this you will need bananas, milk, avocado, watermelon, passion fruit, cocoa powder, chocolate chips, berries, coconut, mint and edible flowers. Raw cocoa bean and red fruits prevent cells from aging. This bowl is really good for your health!

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Risotto with asparagus
programme.desc: In this recipe, we prepare a creamy asparagus risotto, a delicate and refined dish. Arborio rice is slowly simmered in a flavorful broth, then combined with crisp asparagus and melted Parmesan. A hint of lemon and fresh herbs enhances this delicious and comforting meal.

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 1 - Episode 6
programme.desc: In this episode, fly at high altitude with the help of a ULM or with a parachute. Discover underwater wrecks with divers. Take adrenaline on a snowboard, and comtemplate the vastness of the world on the top of a mountain. Don’t miss these hearthtaking landscapes !

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Paul John Elliott
programme.desc: Without really knowing why, Paul Elliott will change his life from cartoonist to massage therapist. Aspired by success, money, success, he will create several clinics and will eventually lose the human aspects and lose himself. A new turn, a new meeting and Paul will finally resume his dreams of painting. Follow Paul in his tumults and his happiness found.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Annabelle Higgins and Valérie Sirois
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Annabelle Higgins and Valérie Sirois
programme.desc: They are beautiful sisters, they are passionate about nature and they have a very strong common point: entrepreneurship. After years of evolution in consumer sectors, discover Annabelle and Valérie who knew how to listen to their passion to create a fitness and surf club (for Annabelle) and a zero waste grocery store (for Valérie).

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Véronick Desmarais
programme.desc: The everyday work is not always easy and it is sometimes difficult to get by. If there is one thing that Véronick has learned from her experience and those around her, it is because she is able to surpass that and to embark on her dreams and plans to become a massage therapist. You will believe in your abilities too.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 2 - Violaine Courtois
programme.desc: Everything started from the dream of her parents, she becomes a veterinarian. In her career that she launched backwards, Violaine will meet people who will take her to a different path of life. The chance will be at the rendezvous but it is especially the courage to resume studies and create his company that will build his project. Follow the encounters and travels of Violaine that brought her to fasciatherapy and nonviolent communication.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Leah
programme.desc: Working 12 hours a day in the tech industry, Leah was living a fast paced high-life that only looked good on paper. Frequently ill and constantly exhausted, she experienced a burn out and decided to tackle meditation. What came next was a radical change in lifestyle that resulted in a move to Bali, The Island of the Gods. Today she sees her career blossom as she enjoys sharing her meditation knowledge with the world.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The wine route
programme.desc: We are in the middle of the harvest season, a perfect opportunity to discover the most beautiful French vineyards. Our trip starts with a bit of tourism. We will cross the Drôme with Tain l'Hermitage and its Baume de Venise, but also the Bordeaux region where the different domains compete to attract this new clientele. Then we head for Épernay, capital of the Champagne region, where the reputation of its sparkling wine is well established. Julie will take us to Burgundy, where the most expensive wines in the world are produced, and then back to Bordeaux, to immerse ourselves in the world of high-tech and biodynamic wines.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - winter vegetables
programme.desc: Summer is the season when vegetables abound in the garden, but it would be a shame not to savor those that the cooler seasons have to offer: soups, pies, stews or gratins highlight seasonal vegetables, perfect for a nice warm up. To begin this new journey, Julie will cook salsify. This little tour of France will also allow us to meet gardening goldsmiths who sculpt winter vegetables and fruits. Finally, we will conclude as usual with a recipe from a chef, who will go out of his way to create a seasonal recipe for us.

Different folding and sculpting techniques, new models, bright colors – you’ll see all of this in our program! We will teach you how to transform a flat square sheet of paper into a finished sculpture – from simple to complex!

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 1 - The Earth does not belong to Man, Man belongs to the Earth - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 The Earth does not belong to Man, Man belongs to the Earth - Part 2
programme.desc: In Florida, the Everglades swamp is being restored at great expense. 15 billion dollars are being invested over thirty years to save the water supply of the entire region. In California, on the crests of the Tehachapi hills, the world's largest wind farm is being discovered. In China, the fermentation of excrement produces gas, mainly methane, which, when burned, is transformed into energy called biogas. In California, the Taft oil field was discovered, one of the largest in the world: nearly 10,000 wells on over 52,000 hectares. Then Haiti fights to save itself from deforestation. In Utah, Salt Lake City is a good example of how we live today: there are 37 cities in the world with more...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Full Moon Ceremony
programme.desc: Today, Val will be heading to Roxy's Opening Ceremony concept store where she will be attending the "full moon ceremony". The session will take place in the intimate garden of the store. During this ceremony, expect all negative thoughts to slowly fade away so you can fully enjoy the positive emotions. Then Val will go to the Beverly Hills Hotel for a Vinyasa Yoga session in a green paradise in the heart of the city.

programme.desc: In this episode, dive underwater with fishes in their natural space near the coast, navigate in the middle of the ocean with a jet ski. Fly above the clouds with a wingsuit and face the water currents with a rafting team. Don’t miss these hearthtaking landscapes !

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 1 - Episode 8
programme.desc: In this episode, slide on the snow with a snowmobile, take a buggy ride in the desert. Dive in the void with a parachutist team and contemplate a sunset at the edge of the Grand Canynon. Don’t miss these hearthtaking landscapes !

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 1 - Episode 9
programme.desc: In this episode, we will climb a mountain covered by snow, follow an extrem skier who goes down the mountain at high speed. Surf in a calm sea, and face the void with a mountain climber. Don’t miss these hearthtaking landscapes !

programme.title: Earth in Sports - Season 1 - Episode 10
programme.desc: In this episode, under a blazing sun, you will have a hike in the moutain. Or also spend the day on the ocean while practicing wakeboarding from sunrise to sunset. Don’t miss these hearthtaking landscapes !

programme.title: Detox minute - Season 1 - Anise orange

programme.title: Vegetarian Delights - Season 1 - Salad with kifferi and pickled peppers
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Salad with kifferi and pickled peppers
programme.desc: Today, we're preparing a pasta salad with pickled peppers. Pasta, tomatoes, carrots and Parmesan are combined with pickled peppers for a tangy, fragrant touch, tomatoes and baby greens. A seasoning of olive oil, garlic and rosemary enhances this fresh, gourmet dish.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 3 - Andrew
programme.desc: For many years, Andrew was a successful American child-actor on the popular show My Wife and Kids, until he dropped everything and embarked on a journey to find life’s true meaning. He now lives in Bali where he coaches people from all over the world on how to radically transform their diets. His 360° transformative journey includes meditation, yoga and nutrition.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Carine (Nice)
programme.desc: After twenty years as a lawyer, Carine decided to give in to her desire to help others heal. Her inner journey led her to discover a passion for naturopathy as she rediscovered her love for nature and learned the healing qualities of the plants around her. Combined with a deep understanding of astrology and nutrition, she is now able to help others live better and find their own path.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Virginie (Lyon)
programme.desc: Virginie’s beautiful handwritten lettering decorates the walls and windows of many shops in and around Lyon, France, as she lives out her passion of finding beauty in every stroke. A former waitress, Virginie was struggling to find her calling when she suffered a life-altering horse accident that almost left her handicapped. At only 30, she was forced to put her whole life into perspective. She finally found herself through a love for graphic design and lettering, combined with a desire for freedom that came with working independently. To motivate other women, she has also launched “Ladyboss”, a network of inspiring women entrepreneurs.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Laetitia (French Alps)
programme.desc: While pregnant with her daughter, Laetitia, a former journalist, experienced a strong desire to change lifestyles and move to the countryside. Remembering the teachings of her grandmother, she began to venture outdoors into the surrounding Alps and study the different plants at her fingertips. Today she holds a wealth of knowledge about the medicinal and aromatherapeutic value of plants that she applies to making natural products. With utmost respect for the environment, Laetitia gathers only what she needs in order to make herbal infusions.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The mushroom route
programme.desc: Today, Julie takes us on a mushroom tour. There is nothing better to occupy your weekends than a walk in the forest in search of edible mushrooms. Some species, such as the cep or the chanterelle, can be preserved very well in the freezer or, for the more patient, in jars. We will also meet two chefs who are passionate about mushrooms and have become specialists in the subject: Franck Quinton and Régis Marcon. But for the moment, we're on the road, discovering typically French dishes with astonishing button mushrooms in Touraine, morels in the Jura, ceps in Gironde, russules charbonnières in Normandy and coulemelles in Auvergne!

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - Truffle flavors
programme.desc: It is a mushroom that we are going to focus on today. A mushroom, or a black diamond, some would say: the truffle. And along the way, we will twist the neck of some clichés. Truffle hunting with dogs, with a sow but also flies... markets and of course cooking to discover or rediscover this mysterious mushroom, in all its aspects, in a salty but also sweet version... Richerenches, Carpentras, Saint Avère, Toulouse, Aix en Provence… the south of France will honor this black diamond!

programme.title: Audio advices - Season 1 - Flat stomach

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 1 - Everything is living and everything is linked - Part 1
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Everything is living and everything is linked - Part 1
programme.desc: Through the most magnificent images and astonishing human stories, Man is at the heart of the biodiversity that he must respect and preserve. We find ourselves in Kenya, where the biodiversity is particularly rich, in the Massaï Mara reserve. Then, in the Democratic Republic of Congo, where the worrying disappearance of hippos from Lake Edward has unexpected consequences for the survival of fishermen. Then, in the sanctuary of life, the Pantanal region in Brazil, where millions of animal and plant species are teeming, some of the world's great wetlands also in danger. In Tasmania, Yann Arthus-Bertrand fights against the napalm deforestation of one of the oldest and most beautiful forests...

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Paddle Yoga
programme.desc: ValKahl tries out the addictive and zen yoga lesson on water on a paddleboard with YOGAqua, in Marina Del Rey. After learning to make some delightful and healthy wrap in restaurant KYE’S, she does sightseeing in Malibu Wine Hikes, the organic grapes garden behind Malibu’s famous beaches. Along the way, she can’t resist biting a mustard plant, feeding and ‘french kissing’ Stanley, the celebrity giraffe. She later harvests some fresh organic vegetables in Saddle Rock Garden!

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Juliette (Morbihan)
programme.desc: Juliette has long been aware of the delicate beauty of nature. For years she spent her life on the sea, organising regattas for the traditional sailboats that are famous in the French region of Morbihan. Her appreciation for the elements made for a smooth transition as she decided to leave the water behind and return to the earth. Learning to shape a ball of clay into a graceful and elegant object, she discovered a passion for ceramics. Today, she lives from her creations and makes pieces that reflect their surroundings, the stunning Gulf of Morbihan.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Anna (Brittany)
programme.desc: Anna’s unusual background provided inspiration for her life change. Her career began as a wolf trainer for cinema and it was during these years that she developed her deep love for animals. After being tired of seeing them used as props and constantly in cages, she decided to leave the world of show business behind and start an eco-friendly sheep and goat pasture. To further share her passion for these gentle animals, Anna has opened a conservatory farm where visitors can experience first-hand the joys of being close to nature. She also makes amazing goat cheese!

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Cristina (Spain)
programme.desc: At 31 years old, the last thing Cristina expected when she went to see the doctor about a blocked ear was to leave the consultation with a diagnosis for Breast Cancer. This young and passionate sociologist decided to apply the same philosophy to her treatment as she did when surfing: when a wave crashes down on you, get up and try again. Through her healing journey she deepened her love for the ocean and also discovered the therapeutic capabilities of yoga for cancer patients. Today she shares her practice through surf and yoga retreats and is specialised in oncological yoga, helping others heal.

programme.title: My Zen People - Season 4 - Idoia (Spain)
programme.desc: Idoia thrives on weaving together the sources of inspiration that surround her. Heavily influenced by the natural beauty of her home region of Galicia, Spain, she creates unique woven pieces from diverse and beautiful materials. Idoia’s pieces represent her vision of a modern woman, drawing from the traditions and experiences of those who perfected the craft before her and also pushing to innovate and adapt her creations for the modern world. Her recent collaboration with luxury fashion house Loewe is a dream come true for this modern artisan.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The Auxerre country
programme.desc: Auxerre is cut by valleys whose best exposed slopes are home to vineyards. The meandering course of the Yonne runs vertically through this region. To the east of Auxerre, the Chablis region is rounded with hills. The vineyard, extending on both sides of the valley of the Serein. To the south, lined by the Nivernais canal, the upper Yonne valley attracts climbing enthusiasts and boaters, before being swollen by the waters of the Cure. The region is renowned throughout the world for the quality of its wines, particularly those of Chablis. Julie begins her journey in Auxerre and then proposes typical recipes from the area: croquignoles and cherry clafoutis.

programme.title: Julie's Frenchy Cookbook - Season 4 - The chestnut route
programme.desc: Julie takes to the road in her little red car to honour one of her favourite fruits: the chestnut. Julie will meet several producers in Ardèche, cook the traditional soup of the region with the sparkling Marithé. She will also make the recipe for homemade marrons glacés, before heading to Corsica to work with chestnut flour and the leaves of her tree. This journey will end as always with a clever chef's recipe, and this time it will be Claude Brioude who will do it! This chef likes to shake up the rules and will work the chestnut with fish, two seasonal products that we are not usually used to combining.

programme.desc: Origami is about the art of paper folding, a Japanese and Chinese millenary tradition. In modern usage, the word “origami” is used as an inclusive term for all paper folding, regardless of their cultural origin. Different folding and sculpting techniques, new models, bright colors – you’ll see all of this in our program! We will teach you how to transform a flat square sheet of paper into a finished sculpture – from simple to complex!

programme.title: Earth from above - Season 1 - Everything is living and everything is linked - Part 2
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Everything is living and everything is linked - Part 2
programme.desc: Through the most magnificent images and amazing human stories, Man is at the heart of the biodiversity that he must respect and preserve. We go to China in the province of Yunan, where a plant with exceptional virtues against malaria has been discovered. Then we travel to Africa, where man's intervention in the ecosystem of Lake Victoria has triggered a considerable imbalance. We will also explore to Greenland to meet the polar bears, the first victims of the melting ice.

programme.title: My Zen Trendy - Season 1 - Crystal healing & Yoguitar
programme.desc: In order to find serenity, Val Kahl experiments meditation with the healing effect of crystals, as well as a Yoguitar class where one does yoga while someone plays live music. programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2021
programme.desc: If one knows in the West above all Iranian moral rigour and war rhetoric, the enduro professionals Anita and Caro Gehrig experience open people, hospitality, great interest in the West and last but not least unconditional passion for mountain biking.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2021
programme.sub-title: S01 : E26 Line & Air - with Geraldine Fasnacht
programme.desc: Swiss snowboard legend Géraldine Fasnacht is an experienced pilot. She invites two other freeride greats, Léo Slemett and Emilien Badoux, to join her riding several couloirs at some glaciers of the Swiss and French Alps.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2021
programme.sub-title: S01 : E28 Kayak Expedition Norway Whitewater Polar Circle
programme.desc: Today’s episode is an exciting show about a unique kayak expedition to Norway’s white-water treasures at the Arctic Circle. Expect remote mountains, raging rivers and challenging rapids!

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2017
programme.sub-title: S01 : E11 Alaska or Bus´t - MTB Roadtrip - Athlete Profile Andrew Taylor (USA)
programme.desc: laska has always been a dream-riding destination for 28-year-old American mountain biker Andrew Taylor. But being over 3,000 miles away from his hometown Santa Cruz, it seemed a bit too far to just jump in the truck and go.

programme.title: Freeride World Tour 2023
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 Freeride World Tour 2023 - stop #1 - Baqueira Beret (ESP)
programme.desc: The 2023 Freeride World Tour kicks off in January in its 16th season – as usual with the best freeriders all over the globe! The first stop of the tour takes place in Baqueira Beret.

programme.title: WOF Evergreens
programme.sub-title: S01 : E01 5 Peaks in One Day - Mountaineering
programme.desc: Zermatt, Switzerland - Swiss alpinist Andreas Steindl (26) has accomplished what most people deem impossible: he ran 30.66 kilometres from Zermatt to Saas Fee crossing five 4000m peaks. in only 7 hours, 45 minutes and 44 seconds.

programme.title: WOF Evergreens
programme.sub-title: S01 : E09 Go Big or Go Home - Freestyle Kayak
programme.desc: Go big or go home - in search of the perfect waveadidas Outdoorsportsteam: Nick Troutman, Mat Dumoulin, Gerd Serrasolses & Peter CsonkaNo distance is too great for these four athletes - their quest takes them and their kayaks from Africa to North America.

programme.title: WOF Evergreens
programme.sub-title: S01 : E12 Mammut Biggest Peak Project 1
programme.desc: Anniversary base camp / Jungfraujoch (Switzerland)On 3 August 2011, exactly 200 years after the first ascent, the Swiss mountain sports outfitter Mammut will be organizing the biggest summit project in the history of mountaineering on the “Top of Europe”...

programme.title: WOF Evergreens
programme.sub-title: S01 : E14 Biggest Peak Project in History - Part III (Cerro Kishtwar)
programme.desc: Mammut 150 Peaks Project: Cerro KishtwarKashmir, India, holds some of the world’s most spectacular and mystical mountains. Now with special permissions, an European team will attempt to climb the most aesthetic one of all, Cerro Kishtwar.

programme.title: Red Bull Romaniacs 2025 - 150min Live Summary
programme.desc: RB Romaniacs challenges hundreds of dedicated amateur participants to compete alongside the world's best professionals. They gather in Sibiu, Romania. Participants come from over 60 countries and continents around the world.

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2018

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2025
programme.sub-title: S01 : E33 WOF 2025 #33: World Downhill Skateboarding Championships - Erzincan (TUR)
programme.desc: The second round of the Downhill Skateboarding World Championship took place under sunny skies high up on Mount Ergan Dağı in Erzincan, Turkey. This was the third visit to the 3 km long track, often referred to as the most difficult in the world.

programme.title: Cape Epic 2025

programme.sub-title: S01 : E27 WOF 2024 #27: World E-Bike Series Round #1 & #2

programme.sub-title: S01 : E38 WOF 2024 #38: Ötztal Cycle Marathon (AUT)

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2024
programme.sub-title: S01 : E44 WOF 2024 #44: Titan Desert Series 2024 - Almeria (ESP)

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2023
programme.sub-title: S01 : E44 WOF 2023 #44: World Rowing Beach Sprint Finals 2023
programme.desc: In der heutigen Folge haben wir ein besonderes Format der World Rowing Tour. In Barletta, Italien, finden die Beach Sprint Finals von 2023 statt. Auf dem Mittelmeer kämpfen die Athleten um den Titel des diesjährigen Champions. Schauen wir mal rein!

programme.title: WORLD OF FREESPORTS 2023
programme.sub-title: S01 : E46 WOF 2023 #46: GKA Kitesurfing World Tour - The Stars of Wave
